data.title: Kenvue gains on report activist Starboard nominates five for board
data.description:
Kenvue (NYSE:KVUE) rose 1.3% after a report that activist Starboard Value has privately nominated five candidates to the Tylenol maker's board.

Starboard and Kenvue (KVUE) have been in discussions about the company's issues, according to a Bloomberg report on Thursday, which cited people familiar with the matter.

Starboard disclosed in October that the fund accumulated a stake and was pushing for changes to help the company's stock price. Kenvue needs to focus on fixing its skin, health and beauty segments, Jeff Smith, founder and CEO of Starboard Value said at the time after he presented at an activist conference.

Starboard highlighted in a presentation in October that it believes the skin, health and beauty segment, mainly known for its Neutrogena and Aveeno brands, can return its margins to FY19 levels, which would be in line with peers. Kenvue (KVUE) was spun off from Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) last year.

“The Kenvue board and management team are committed to acting in the best interests of the company and our shareholders, and in pursuit of this shared objective, we maintain ongoing dialogue with our shareholders,” a company spokesperson said in a statement to Bloomberg.

data.title: JPMorgan plans to hire 1,500 for hub in Argentina
data.description:
JPMorgan Chase (NYSE:JPM) plans to expand its Buenos Aires corporate center as one of its global hubs for technology, finance, operations, and legal services, hiring 1,500 workers over the next five years, according to a media report.

The global bank is "working on a longer-term real estate plan to accommodate" the new hires in addition to the 3,500 employees who already work for JPM in the Argentine capital, Alfonso Eyzaguirre, CEO of J.P. Morgan Latin America and Canada and head of Latin America investment banking, told Bloomberg News in an interview.

JPMorgan Chase's (NYSE:JPM) move to combine its commercial bank with its corporate and investment bank globally, will help the company grow in Latin America.

"Now that we’re totally integrated, we will be able to serve clients of the legacy commercial bank in countries in Latin America where we were not covering them,” Eyzaguirre said.

Before the integration, JPMorgan's (JPM) Latin American commercial bank business only operated in Brazil and Mexico. Its business, which serves businesses that range from startups to firms with more than $2B in annual revenue, can now deepen coverage in the region, he said.

J.P. Morgan has operated in Argentina for more than 125 years and has participated in most of the country's landmark transactions through advising local and international corporate clients and providing financing for the Argentine government, the company said on its website.

data.title: Wall Street slips after producer inflation data comes in hot
data.description:
U.S. stocks were largely lower on Thursday, following some key producer inflation data that came in hotter than expected.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last -0.14% in morning trade, while the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -0.27%. The blue-chip Dow (DJI) was flat.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was up 3 basis points to 4.30%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) was up nearly 2 basis points to 4.17%.

Before the start of regular trading, the November Producer Price Index (PPI) came in at +0.4% M/M vs. +0.3% consensus, and +3.0% Y/Y vs. +2.6%. Core PPI came in at +0.2% M/M vs. +0.2% consensus, and +3.4% Y/Y vs. +3.2% consensus.

Separately, initial jobless claims unexpectedly jumped in the past week.

U.S. stocks finished mostly higher in Wednesday's history-making session, as an in-line consumer inflation report bolstered expectations of another quarter-point rate cut by the Federal Reserve next week. Nasdaq had ended above 20K, with the Big Tech boosting the index.

"Markets put in a decent performance yesterday, as the U.S. CPI report came in broadly as expected, which was seen as giving the all-clear for the Federal Reserve to cut rates next week," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

"When it comes to the Fed, the CPI print left little doubt among investors that another rate cut will happen next week," Allen added.

Adobe (ADBE) shares fell 10.2% after the Photoshop creator issued guidance for fiscal 2025 that fell short of Wall Street's expectations.

More on markets

  • Behind the monster rally that drove the Nasdaq to 20,000
  • CPI Report: Inflation Is No Longer A Concern
  • Reaction Roundup: Experts weigh in on November consumer inflation report
  • The Recipe For A Bull Market

data.title: Keros stock plunges 76% on drug study halt over safety concerns
data.description:
Keros Therapeutics (NASDAQ:KROS) stock plunged 76% Thursday after the company said it has halted two arms of a Phase 2 clinical study of its drug cibotercept in patients with pulmonary arterial hypertension, or PAH, over safety concerns.

The company said it halted dosing in the 3.0 mg/kg and 4.5 mg/kg treatment arms of its TROPOS Phase 2 study due to the observation of pericardial effusion adverse events. Dosing in the 1.5 mg/kg arm is ongoing following a risk/benefit assessment by the independent data monitoring committee, according to a statement.

The Phase 2 study is evaluating cibotercept, also known as KER-012, in combination with background therapy in patients with pulmonary arterial hypertension. The company plans to present data from all treatment arms in Q2 2025.

Keros (KROS) added that it has informed the FDA and other regulatory authorities about the halt.

data.title: Quoin Pharmaceuticals announces 19.23M equity offering
data.description:
• Late-stage clinical development company Quoin Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:QNRX) to offer up to ~19.23M ordinary shares represented by ADS and warrants.
  • We estimate that we will receive net proceeds of ~$13.4M, based upon an assumed public offering price of $0.78 per ADS and accompanying warrants.
  • The company expects to use proceeds for general corporate purposes.
  • Source: Form S-1 filing

data.title: U.S. selected services revenue gains 2.2% in Q3: Quarterly Services Survey
data.description:
Q3 2024 Quarterly Services Report: U.S. selected services total revenue rose 2.2% Q/Q $5,735.4B, accelerating from the 1.6% revised increase in Q2, the U.S. Census Bureau said on Thursday. The latest figure was also up 8.4% from a year earlier.

Utilities revenue for Q3 decreased 1.4% Q/Q and advanced 3.2% Y/Y to $186.0B.

Transportation and warehousing revenue of $353.5B rose 1.2% from the previous three-month period and 4.8% from last year's Q3.

Information sector revenue was $613.5B, up 2.2% from Q2 and 7.7% Y/Y.

Finance and insurance revenue of $1,757.0B was 2.1% higher from Q2 and 9.5% higher from Q3 2023.

Real estate and rental leasing revenue increased 1.4% Q/Q and 6.6% Y/Y to $262.5B.

Health care and social assistance revenue stood at $1,020.7B, up 3.7% sequentially and 12.1% from a year before, the Census Bureau said.

data.title: Visa Direct to speed funds availability for U.S. cardholders
data.description:
Visa's (NYSE:V) direct payment service will make funds available to U.S. cardholders' bank accounts within a minute or less starting in April 2025, the company said Thursday.

Consumers, businesses, and governments can use Visa Direct to deposit funds to bank accounts linked to eligible debit cards in real time.

The faster funds availability can enhance organizations' operational efficiency, deliver better customer experiences, and foster trust among their clients, Visa (NYSE:V) said.

Visa (V) stock edged up 0.1% in Thursday premarket trading.

data.title: Gold set for modest growth in 2025, WGC says, China will be key
data.description:
With gold prices (XAUUSD:CUR) set for their best annual performance in more than a decade, the Federal Reserve and the direction of the U.S. dollar will continue to be important drivers for the asset in 2025, the World Gold Council said.

While lower yields and a weakening U.S. dollar in Q3 fueled Western investment flows, it is gold’s role as a hedge amidst rising market volatility and geopolitical risk that most likely explains its remarkable performance, WGC said, as it expects a positive, but "much more modest growth for gold in 2025."

"(Donald) Trump’s second term may provide a boost to the local economy but could equally elicit a fair degree of nervousness for investors around the world," the Gold Outlook 2025 report released on December 12 said.

Bullion has increased by more than 28% since the beginning of the year, and reached 40 record highs, with total gold demand in the third quarter surpassing $100 billion for the first time.

Thrill-seeking investors may benefit from an early wave of risk-on flows, but potential trade wars and inflationary forces may spill over into an expected subpar economic growth, the report suggests.

An upside could come from stronger-than-expected central bank demand, or from a rapid deterioration of financial conditions leading to flight-to-quality flows, the report said.

Conversely, a reversal in monetary policy, leading to higher interest rates, is seen bringing challenges.

Overall, gold is likely to remain range bound if existing market expectations are correct.

However, a combination of higher rates and lower economic growth could negatively affect investors and consumers. This could be particularly evident in Asia, WGC concluded, adding that China’s contribution to the gold market will be key.
